Question: Determine whether the ordered pair is a solution to the system. $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x - 5 y > 10 \\ 2 x + 3 y > -2\end{matrix}\right .$$ ⓐ $$( 3 , -1 )$$ ⓑ $$( 6 , -3 )$$
Determine whether the ordered pair is a solution to the system. $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}y > 4 x - 2 \\ 4 x - y < 20\end{matrix}\right .$$ ⓐ $$( 2 , 1 )$$ ⓑ $$( 4 , -1 )$$
Solve the system by graphing. $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}y < 3 x + 2 \\ y > \text{unicode[:-]} x - 1\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the system by graphing. $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}y < - \frac{1}{2} x + 3 \\ y < 3 x - 4\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the system by graphing. $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x + y \le 2 \\ y \geq \frac{2}{3} x - 1\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the system by graphing. $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x - 2 y \le 6 \\ y > - \frac{1}{4} x + 5\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the system by graphing. $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}y \geq 3 x - 2 \\ y < -1\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the system by graphing. $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x > -4 \\ x - 2 y \le - 4\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the system by graphing. $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x - 2 y \le 12 \\ y \geq \frac{3}{2} x + 1\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the system by graphing. $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x + 3 y > 8 \\ y < - \frac{1}{3} x - 2\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the system by graphing. $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}y \geq 3 x + 1 \\ -3 x + y \geq -4\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the system by graphing. $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}y \le - \frac{1}{4} x + 2 \\ x + 4 y \le 4\end{matrix}\right .$$

A trailer can carry a maximum weight of 160 pounds and a maximum volume of 15 cubic feet. A microwave oven weighs 30 pounds and has 2 cubic feet of volume, while a printer weighs 20 pounds and has 3 cubic feet of space. ⓐ Write a system of inequalities to model this situation. ⓑ Graph the system. ⓒ Could 4 microwaves and 2 printers be carried on this trailer? ⓓ Could 7 microwaves and 3 printers be carried on this trailer?
Answer: ⓐ $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}30 m + 20 p \le 160 \\ 2 m + 3 p \le 15\end{matrix}\right .$$ ⓑ ⓒ yes ⓓ no

Question: Mary needs to purchase supplies of answer sheets and pencils for a standardized test to be given to the juniors at her high school. The number of the answer sheets needed is at least 5 more than the number of pencils. The pencils cost $2 and the answer sheets cost $1. Mary’s budget for these supplies allows for a maximum cost of $400. ⓐ Write a system of inequalities to model this situation. ⓑ Graph the system. ⓒ Could Mary purchase 100 pencils and 100 answer sheets? ⓓ Could Mary purchase 150 pencils and 150 answer sheets? ⓐ Write a system of inequalities to model this situation. ⓑ Graph the system. ⓒ Could Mary purchase 100 pencils and 100 answer sheets? ⓓ Could Mary purchase 150 pencils and 150 answer sheets?

Tenison needs to eat at least an extra 1,000 calories a day to prepare for running a marathon. He has only $25 to spend on the extra food he needs and will spend it on $0.75 donuts which have 360 calories each and $2 energy drinks which have 110 calories. ⓐ Write a system of inequalities that models this situation. ⓑ Graph the system. ⓒ Can he buy 8 donuts and 4 energy drinks? ⓓ Can he buy 1 donut and 3 energy drinks?
Answer: ⓐ $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}0.75 d + 2 e \le 25 \\ 360 d + 110 e \geq 1000\end{matrix}\right .$$ ⓑ ⓒ yes ⓓ no

Question: Philip’s doctor tells him he should add at least 1000 more calories per day to his usual diet. Philip wants to buy protein bars that cost $1.80 each and have 140 calories and juice that costs $1.25 per bottle and have 125 calories. He doesn’t want to spend more than $12. ⓐ Write a system of inequalities that models this situation. ⓑ Graph the system. ⓒ Can he buy 3 protein bars and 5 bottles of juice? ⓒ Can he buy 5 protein bars and 3 bottles of juice?
Answer: ⓐ $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}140 p + 125 j \geq 1000 \\ 1.80 p + 1.25 j \le 12\end{matrix}\right .$$ ⓑ ⓒ yes ⓓ no
